successCriteria.description: src/modules/balance/balance.model.ts exists (from Phase 6) and exports BalanceStatus enum with values ACTIVE and EXHAUSTED

src/modules/balance/balance.service.ts exists and exports ILeaveBalanceService interface and BalanceService class with methods: getBalance(employeeId, leaveTypeId), getBalancesByEmployee(employeeId), deductBalance(employeeId, leaveTypeId, days), restoreBalance(employeeId, leaveTypeId, days), initializeBalance(employeeId, policyId, fiscalYear)
src/modules/balance/balance.controller.ts exists and exports BalanceController with handlers getBalances, getBalancesByEmployee, initializeBalance that delegate to ILeaveBalanceService

src/modules/balance/balance.routes.ts exists and exports balanceRoutes function that registers GET /balances, GET /balances/:employeeId, POST /balances/initialize with Zod validation on POST body and RBAC preHandler on all routes
All balance mutations (deductBalance, restoreBalance, initializeBalance) execute the balance update and audit record insertion atomically within a single database transaction
Jest unit tests in tests/unit/modules/balance/ cover all service methods, mock repository and audit service, and pass
All async errors in controller handlers are caught and mapped to appropriate HTTP error responses (GP-006)
Input validation is applied at the API boundary using Zod schemas in route definitions (GP-003)

constraints: Repository pattern: All database access goes through repository interfaces.
Audit records: All state-changing operations write an audit record.
Input validation: Validate all inputs at API boundaries before processing.
RBAC enforcement: All API endpoints enforce role-based access control.
Error handling: No unhandled promise rejections. All async errors are caught and handled.
outOfScope: Phase 10 - Leave service, controller, and routes
Any UI components or frontend changes
